title: Frequency Maps, Counter Semantics, and Reconciliation · Program Brief: Frequency Reconciliation
summary: A counting loop resets existing entries, aliases a shared dictionary, includes empty tokens, sorts while mutating, and never reconciles token totals with frequency totals. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the frequency reconciliation increment in inspectable frequency engine. Learners progress through predict…
objectives: Assert that nonnegative frequencies sum to the accepted token count and preserve excluded-token evidence.
Return a fresh result or an explicitly owned mutable result and prevent hidden cross-run state.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Predict Frequency Reconciliation · Theory Frequency Reconciliation
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Bookbot team handles case 8588 during a large-file migration rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to produce an owned frequency map whose nonnegative counts reconcile to accepted tokens; retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity; then split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-frequency-counter-4
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-frequency-counter-4.
# Case 1655: an offline public-library reading report.
# Operating constraint: test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output.
# Changed case: move the input through a symlink outside the allowed root and reject it without reading content.
def worked_bookbot_frequency_counter_4(token_count: int = 12, frequency_total: int = 12, vocabulary: int = 5, negative_counts: int = 0, owned_result: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "corpusworked4",
        "policy4dekrow",
        "revision1655",
        "commandworked41655",
        "report4dekrow1655",
        "test1655worked4",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if token_count < 0 or vocabulary < 0:
        return False, token_count, "count-shape"
    if negative_counts > 0:
        return False, negative_counts, "negative-frequency"
    if frequency_total != token_count:
        return False, frequency_total, "reconciliation"
    if not owned_result:
        return False, vocabulary, "aliased-result"
    assert vocabulary <= token_count
    return True, vocabulary, "reconciled-counter"

Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Assert that nonnegative frequencies sum to the accepted token count and preserve excluded-token evidence.

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Frequency Reconciliation model · Theory Frequency Reconciliation, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: A plausible most-common list proves all tokens were counted.
